data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T10 — Infrastructure / IaC overlay), focused on the LAST checklist item: human/admin authentication to every network-reachable service the plan stands up. The overlay APPLIES (real Terraform-provisioned cloud infra + a stood-up, internet-facing Gerrit code-review server). ONE grounded finding — the human-auth contract for Gerrit itself. (Prior T10/T5c reviews on this ticket covered state/IAM/blast-radius/observability and the EBS at-rest gap; I do NOT re-raise those.)

FINDING (1):

- [ ] Specify how HUMAN principals (developers AND admins) authenticate to the internet-facing Gerrit web UI / REST (:443) and Gerrit SSH (:29418) — a named identity mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) OR an explicit, justified no-auth rationale that is safe for an internet-exposed box. As written the plan stands up Gerrit on a public surface (`network.tf`: 443 and 29418 ingress `cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]`, an Elastic IP + public DNS `rebar.solutions.navateam.com`) but the ONLY auth mode actually configured is `auth.type = DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` (`infra/compose/gerrit.config:75`), under which any anonymous visitor can "become ANY account" — including the Administrators group (the first dev-login account 1000000 is auto-added to Administrators; see `bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15,35`). That is not authentication — it is anonymous admin assumption reachable from the whole internet, which also defeats the plan's core integrity claim (the `LLM-Review` gate + submit-requirement can be self-voted / bypassed by anyone who "becomes" a Service User or admin). The plan's own config comment flags this as unresolved: "PoC ONLY ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive ... S3/S4a may refine to a real auth type (OAuth/HTTP) once the bootstrap is done" (`gerrit.config:70-74`), and `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md:219` confirms the live box still runs `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT`. No plan section, AC, or ADR states the human-auth mechanism (a repo-wide `OAuth`/`auth.type` search under infra/ shows only the DEVELOPMENT setting and the deferral comment). NOTE: the machine credentials the plan does specify (deploy key, bot HTTP token, SSM secrets) authenticate MACHINES, not human/admin access to Gerrit — per T10 they do not satisfy this sub-check. This is a deny-by-default under-specified (here, affirmatively unsafe) human-auth contract on an internet-reachable service.
  - criteria: [T10]
  - location: AC1 ("reverse proxy on 443; Gerrit HTTP 8080 + SSH 29418"); infra/terraform/network.tf:27-55 (0.0.0.0/0 on 443 + 29418); infra/compose/gerrit.config:69-75; infra/scripts/bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15,34-37; docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md:219
  - evidence: code fact — `auth.type = DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` is the ONLY configured Gerrit auth mode (gerrit.config:75); network.tf opens 443 + 29418 to `0.0.0.0/0` with a public EIP + DNS; the first dev-login is auto-added to Administrators (bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh:15) → any anonymous internet visitor can assume admin; plan/config phrase "S3/S4a MAY refine to a real auth type ... do NOT leave this on a box that holds anything sensitive" (gerrit.config:72-74) = explicitly deferred/unresolved; ABSENCE of any named human-auth mechanism (OAuth/OIDC/SSO/LDAP/HTTP-auth) in any AC/ADR/plan section; T10 last checklist item (human + admin auth on every stood-up network-reachable service; service-to-service creds do NOT count).
  - suggested_fix: Add an AC + ADR to S2 (or S3/S4a) that pins Gerrit's human-auth posture BEFORE the box is internet-exposed: EITHER (a) set `auth.type` to a real IdP (e.g. `OAUTH` via the gerrit-oauth-provider plugin / GitHub or Google OIDC, or `HTTP` behind an authenticating nginx layer) with anonymous access disabled on write; OR (b) if a real IdP is genuinely out of scope for the PoC, restrict the 443 + 29418 ingress `cidr_blocks` to a trusted operator CIDR / VPN / SG-gated allowlist (not `0.0.0.0/0`) and record that network-perimeter restriction as the explicit, justified no-auth rationale — so `DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` is never reachable from the open internet. Whichever is chosen, state it in the plan rather than deferring with "S3/S4a may refine".

AFFIRMATIONS (T10 dimensions this plan satisfies — corroborated by prior reviews + the infra/ code):
- (a) STATE + plan-before-apply: S3 remote backend with native `use_lockfile` locking (no local state, TF >= 1.10 floor), one-time local-state bootstrap for the state bucket; `terraform plan`-before-`apply` reaffirmed epic-wide (esp. the high-stakes S6 ruleset replace) and in S7's runbook AC.
- (b) LEAST-PRIVILEGE IAM: SSM read scoped to `arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/*` (not `*`), `kms:Decrypt` bounded by a `kms:ViaService` condition, single-owner role contract (S1 creates; S2/S4a only ADD scoped inline policies); replication deploy key single-repo write-only. No wildcard admin. No long-lived creds in IaC.
- (c) IDEMPOTENCY & DRIFT: single-owner contracts for the EC2 role, `gerrit.config` (S2 base + S3 `[change]`-only RMW) and the DLM policy (S1 owns; S7 only asserts); IaC-rendered configs, `ignore_changes=[ami]` to avoid churn.
- (d) BLAST RADIUS: `prevent_destroy` on the NoteDb data volume + EIP + state bucket; data volume separate from root; freeze-and-restore + "merged code safe on the GitHub mirror" bound RPO; single-env PoC explicitly accepted. (EBS at-rest encryption gap already raised in prior T5c/T10 findings — not duplicated here.)
- (f) COST & SIZING: single Graviton t4g.large, pinned ~2 GB heap, gp3, default VPC, no ALB/NAT, SSM (free) over Secrets Manager, DLM retain=7.
- (g) OBSERVABILITY & TEARDOWN: Rebar/Gate:GerritReachable metric + alarm, EC2 status-check backstops, SNS→SSM endpoint; reproducible as-code with a restore drill; documented state-bucket teardown.

No other NEW T10 defects — the state/IAM/idempotency/blast-radius/cost/observability dimensions are specified and code-corroborated; the single gap is the unspecified (and currently unsafe) human/admin authentication to the internet-facing Gerrit service.
